alternatives_considered: Build a custom Ageix UI from scratch before proving the governance workflows.
Expose only chat-based interaction and defer structured decision surfaces.
Use Open WebUI as a system of record rather than an interface shell.
Wait for a complete intent engine before starting UI integration.
consequences: Lower reinvention cost for generic UI infrastructure.
Requires a clean Ageix adapter layer.
Open WebUI must not become the Ageix system of record.
All state-changing UI actions must route through governed Ageix capabilities.
The integration spike must validate auth/session boundaries and customization approach before deeper coupling.
context: Ageix Phase 26 introduces human-facing workflows for governed decision review, approvals, feedback, manual workflow initiation, notifications, and future mobile entry points. Ageix already has governed capability execution, project-scoped evidence packages, architecture ADR/principle proposal flows, decision traceability, validation profiles, worker boundaries, and summary-first architecture context. The human interface must reuse those patterns rather than becoming a parallel system of record.
decision: Ageix Phase 26 will use Open WebUI as the initial human interface framework for governed decision review, approvals, feedback, and manual workflow initiation. Ageix-specific governance remains inside Ageix, not inside Open WebUI.
future_considerations: A native app or PWA can be evaluated after notification and lightweight approval-link patterns are proven.
Mobile must remain downstream of the governed web/API model.
Conversation workflows can be expanded after governance workflows are exposed clearly.

acceptance: Open WebUI acts as shell only.
All mutations route through governed capabilities.

rationale: Open WebUI provides an existing extensible interface shell and avoids rebuilding generic UI infrastructure. Using it as a shell accelerates the UI start while preserving Ageix as the authority for proposals, evidence, decisions, validation, artifacts, workers, audit, and governance.
title: Use Open WebUI as the Initial Ageix Human Interface Shell
tradeoffs: Open WebUI may constrain UX patterns compared with a custom interface.
Adapter discipline is required to avoid accidental governance bypass.
Initial UI surfaces should prioritize governance workflows over general conversation convenience.
